zh.lifeSummary: 當Osbern le Scrope於1045年出生在英國賀福爾理查茲 卡素時，他的父親Richard FitzScrope26歲，母親Agnes de FitzRichard36歲。他於1078年與Nest ferch Gruffudd在威爾士福林爾爾德藍結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1100年在英國史洛普爾盧德洛福享年55歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Osbern le Scrope于1045年出生在英国賀福爾理查茲 卡素时，他的父亲Richard FitzScrope26岁，母亲Agnes de FitzRichard36岁。他于1078年与Nest ferch Gruffudd在威尔士福林爾爾德藍结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1100年在英国史洛普爾盧德洛福享年55岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Scrope
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman: of uncertain origin. Tengvik suggests derivation from the Old Scandinavian personal name Skrópi (of doubtful etymology, but compare Old Scandinavian skrópa-maðr ‘hypocrite’, containing the genitive plural form of skrópar ‘sham, hypocrisy’). It is possible, but not certain, that this personal name forms the first element of the Notts place-name Scrooby. Otherwise there is no evidence for its currency in England or, more relevantly, in Normandy. Richards Castle (in Herefs and Shrops) was apparently named after Richard Scrope, who held estates in Herefs and Worcs in 1066 and was a Norman favourite of Edward the Confessor. The Scrope family, whose name is traditionally pronounced /skru:p/, was politically important in medieval England, and acquired estates in Lincs (Barton on Humber), NR Yorks (Castle Bolton), and in ER Yorks (Flotmanby). Richard (le) Scrope of Bolton, in Wensleydale, was Lord Chancellor in the reign of Richard II. The use of le and de in the surname, suggesting either a nickname or a locative name, must be treated with caution because these were often added gratuitously to Anglo-Norman surnames as a sign of high social status.

nameMeanings.name: Osbern
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Late Old English personal name composed of ōs ‘god’ + beorn ‘bear, warrior’ (both of Scandinavian origin). As a modern given name it generally represents a transferred use of the surname that was derived from this name during the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Osbourne
meanings: From a Late Old English personal name composed of ōs ‘god’ + beorn ‘bear, warrior’ (both of Scandinavian origin). As a modern given name it generally represents a transferred use of the surname that was derived from this name during the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Osborne
meanings: From a Late Old English personal name composed of ōs ‘god’ + beorn ‘bear, warrior’ (both of Scandinavian origin). As a modern given name it generally represents a transferred use of the surname that was derived from this name during the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Battle of Hastings
historicEvents.description: Battle of Hastings was fought between the Norman-French army and the battle-wary English army on October 14, 1066. Duke William of Normandy believed he was the rightful king rather than Harold, who had been crowned king several months earlier. Harold was killed in battle and William was crowned king on Christmas Day 1066.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

en.lifeSummary: When Osbern le Scrope was born in 1045, in Richards Castle, Herefordshire, England, his father, Richard FitzScrope, was 26 and his mother, Agnes de FitzRichard, was 36. He married Nest ferch Gruffudd in 1078, in Rhuddlan, Flintshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1100, in Ludlow, Shropshire, England, at the age of 55.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Osbern le Scrope est né en 1045, à Richards Castle, Herefordshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard FitzScrope, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Agnes de FitzRichard, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Nest ferch Gruffudd en 1078, à Rhuddlan, Flintshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1100, à Ludlow, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 55 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Osbern le Scrope 1045 in Richards Castle, Herefordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard FitzScrope 26 und seine Mutter Agnes de FitzRichard 36. Er hat 1078 in Rhuddlan, Flintshire, Wales Nest ferch Gruffudd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1100 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Ludlow, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Osbern le Scrope è nato il nel 1045 nel Richards Castle, Herefordshire, England, il suo padre, Richard FitzScrope, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Agnes de FitzRichard, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Nest ferch Gruffudd nel 1078, nel Rhuddlan, Flintshire, Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1100, nel Ludlow, Shropshire, England, all'età di 55 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Osbern le Scropeが1045年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアリチャーズ カストルで生まれた時，父Richard FitzScropeは26歳，母Agnes de FitzRichardは36歳であった。1078年にウェールズフリントシュアイアルハッドランでNest ferch Gruffuddと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1100年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアルドロウで55歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Osbern le Scrope(이)가 1045년 영국 허퍼드실르 리처스 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard FitzScrope(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Agnes de FitzRichard(은)는 36세였다. 그는 1078년 웨일스 플린츠히르 후들런에서 Nest ferch Gruffudd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1100년 영국 슈럽실르 루들로에서 55세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Osbern le Scrope nasceu em 1045, em Richards Castle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard FitzScrope, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Agnes de FitzRichard, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Nest ferch Gruffudd em 1078, em Rhuddlan, Flintshire, Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1100, em Ludlow, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 55 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Osbern le Scrope родился в 1045 г. в местности ричардс кастл, хирефордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard FitzScrope было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Agnes de FitzRichard, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Nest ferch Gruffudd в 1078 г. в местности руддлан, флинцхир, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1100 г. в местности лудлоу, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 55 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Osbern le Scrope nació en 1045, en Richards Castle, Herefordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard FitzScrope, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Agnes de FitzRichard, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Nest ferch Gruffudd en 1078, en Rhuddlan, Flintshire, Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1100, en Ludlow, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 55 años.
